expectations.text: Contains a concrete analogy using familiar real-world concepts
expectations.evidence: Teaching a toddler to recognize animals — pointing, correcting, pattern learning. Universally familiar.

expectations.text: Explicitly states where the analogy breaks down
expectations.evidence: 'Where this analogy breaks down: A toddler builds rich, flexible understanding... A machine learning model knows none of that. It only learns the narrow pattern you train it on.'

expectations.text: Includes a visual (tldraw, Mermaid, or structured)
expectations.evidence: Mermaid flowchart showing the training feedback loop (Data → Model → Guess → Compare → Adjust → repeat).

expectations.text: The visual teaches something the text alone does not
expectations.evidence: The circular loop structure (adjust feeds back into model) makes the iterative nature visually obvious in a way the linear text doesn't convey.
One-Liner → Analogy → Visual → 'A Bit More Detail' (training, parameters, ML vs. traditional) → Go Deeper.

expectations.text: Includes curated external links with descriptions
expectations.evidence: Four links: 3Blue1Brown neural network video, Google Teachable Machine, TF Playground, Coursera roadmap — each with why-click sentence.

expectations.text: Technical terms introduced only after being tied to familiar concept
expectations.evidence: 'training' = guess-check-adjust cycle, 'parameters' = adjustable knobs, 'gradient descent' mentioned but deferred. Each term anchored to analogy first.

expectations.text: No single section introduces more than 4 new concepts
expectations.evidence: Detail section introduces training, then parameters/weights, then traditional vs. ML. Three concepts, each with its own paragraph.

expectations.text: Acknowledges that machine learning is genuinely complex/broad
expectations.evidence: 'People often confuse machine learning with artificial intelligence. Machine learning is one technique within the broader field of AI.' Also: 'that's a topic for another day' re: gradient descent.
